title: Hidden Forces Inside Earth
summary: Deep inside Earth, there is a layer called the mantle. It moves very slowly like honey. Scientists found that this movement can stretch and twist the rocks. They used earthquake waves to see inside Earth. When earthquakes happen, waves travel through Earth. The waves move differently depending on the rocks they go through. By studying these waves, scientists made a map of the deep mantle. They saw that most of the twisting happens where old pieces of Earth's crust have sunk down. This helps us understand how Earth's inside works.

keywords.explanation: The layer of Earth between the crust and the core.
Energy that travels through Earth when an earthquake happens.

questions.question: What is the mantle?
options: The layer between crust and core
questions.correct_answer: The layer between crust and core

questions.question: How did scientists study the deep mantle?
What happens to the mantle material?
What did scientists make?
options: A map of the deep mantle
questions.correct_answer: A map of the deep mantle

background_read: Earth is made of layers: crust, mantle, and core. The mantle is very hot and moves slowly. Earthquakes happen when rocks break and release energy.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists led by Jonathan Wolf at University of California, Berkeley.
WHAT: They discovered that deep mantle deformation happens where ancient tectonic slabs have sunk.
WHERE: In the lowermost mantle, about 2,900 km below Earth's surface.
WHY: To understand how the mantle flows and deforms.

perspectives.description: This study gives new information about Earth's deep interior.
